entries.definition:
كتاب الواو

وعد - وعر

(وعظ) الواو والعين والظاء: كلمةٌ واحدة. فالوَعْظ: التخويف. والعِظَة الاسمُ منه؛ قال الخليل: هو التَّذكير بالخير وما يرقُّ له قلبُه .

([باب الواو والغين وما يثلثهما])

appointed place, promised destination (11:17) وَمَنْ يَكْفُرْ بِهِ مِنَ الْأَحْزَابِ
فَالنَّارُ مَوْعِدُهُ as for those of the groups who deny it [its truth], the
Fire is their promised place.

مَوْعِدَة mawʿidatun [n.] a solemn promise (9:114) وَمَا كَانَ اسْتِغْفَارُ
إِبْرَاهِيمَ لِأَبِيهِ إِلَّا عَنْ مَوْعِدَةٍ وَعَدَهَا إِيَّاهُ Abraham’s seeking forgiveness
[from God] for his father was only because of a promise he [his
father] had made to him.

مَوْعُود mawʿūd [pass. part.] that which is agreed upon,
promised *(85:2) وَالْيَوْمِ الْمَوْعُودِ by the promised Day, the inevitable
Day.

مِيعَاد mīʿād I [v. n.; n.] a promise (39:20) لَا يُخْلِفُ اللَّهُ الْمِيعَادَ God
does not fail the promise II [n. of place; n. of time] place or time
of an appointment (8:42) لَاخْتَلَفْتُمْ فِي الْمِيعَادِ you would surely have
differed [in keeping the time of, or in finding the place of], the
appointment.
entries.definition_english:
appointed place, promised destination (11:17) وَمَنْ يَكْفُرْ بِهِ مِنَ الْأَحْزَابِ
فَالنَّارُ مَوْعِدُهُ as for those of the groups who deny it [its truth], the

إِبْرَاهِيمَ لِأَبِيهِ إِلَّا عَنْ مَوْعِدَةٍ وَعَدَهَا إِيَّاهُ Abraham’s seeking forgiveness
[from God] for his father was only because of a promise he [his
father] had made to him.

مَوْعُود mawʿūd [pass. part.] that which is agreed upon,
promised *(85:2) وَالْيَوْمِ الْمَوْعُودِ by the promised Day, the inevitable
Day.

مِيعَاد mīʿād I [v. n.; n.] a promise (39:20) لَا يُخْلِفُ اللَّهُ الْمِيعَادَ God
does not fail the promise II [n. of place; n. of time] place or time
of an appointment (8:42) لَاخْتَلَفْتُمْ فِي الْمِيعَادِ you would surely have
differed [in keeping the time of, or in finding the place of], the
appointment.

entries.definition:
w–ʿ–ẓ to admonish, to exhort, to advise, to teach, to learn by
example; an example, to learn from. Of this root, four forms
occur 25 times in the Qur’an: وَعَظ waʿaẓa 11 times; يُوعَظ yūʿaẓ
four times; وَاعِظِين wāʿiẓīn once and مَوْعِظَة mawʿiẓatun nine times.

وَعَظ waʿaẓa i [v. trans.] to admonish, to exhort, to urge
(34:46) إِنَّمَا أَعِظُكُمْ بِوَاحِدَةٍ I only impress upon you one thing.

يُوعَظ yūʿaẓ [pass. imperf.] 1 to be admonished, to be exhorted,
to be commanded, to be urged (4:66) وَلَوْ أَنَّهُمْ فَعَلُوا مَا يُوعَظُونَ بِهِ لَكَانَ
خَيْرًا لَهُمْ yet if they had done what they were admonished to do, it
would have been better for them 2 to be taught, to be guided, to
be made to take heed (65:2) ذَلِكُمْ يُوعَظُ بِهِ مَنْ كَانَ يُؤْمِنُ بِاللَّهِ وَالْيَوْمِ الْآخِرِ
thus are guided those who believe in God and the Last Day.

وَاعِظِين wāʿiẓīn [pl. of act. part. وَاعِظ wāʿiẓ] those who
admonish, preachers (26:136) سَوَاءٌ عَلَيْنَا أَوَعَظْتَ أَمْ لَمْ تَكُنْ مِنَ الْوَاعِظِينَ it
makes no difference to us whether you preach or are not one of
those who preach.

مَوْعِظَة mawʿiẓatun [n./v. n.] 1 a warning, a lesson to learn from
(2:66) فَجَعَلْنَاهَا نَكَالًا لِمَا بَيْنَ يَدَيْهَا وَمَا خَلْفَهَا وَمَوْعِظَةً لِلْمُتَّقِينَ so We made this

1035

1035
